Implements a window for action chaining with menu and status bar layers in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
The code defines a module for managing a user interface window that displays a menu of actions, specifically for a Pebble smartwatch application. The module is centered around the `ChainingWindowData` structure, which holds the window, menu layer, status bar layer, and callback functions for handling user interactions. The primary functionality is to create and manage a window that displays a list of actions from a `TimelineItemActionGroup`, allowing users to select actions and trigger associated callbacks.

The module includes functions to initialize and configure the menu layer, handle user selections, and manage the lifecycle of the window. The [`action_chaining_window_push`](<#action_chaining_window_push>) function is the entry point for creating and displaying the window. It initializes the `ChainingWindowData` structure, sets up the window handlers, and pushes the window onto the window stack. The code supports both round and rectangular displays, with conditional compilation directives to adjust the UI elements accordingly. The module provides a specific interface for integrating action chaining functionality into a Pebble application, allowing developers to define custom behavior when users interact with the menu.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `action_chaining_window.h`
- `applib/ui/ui.h`
- `kernel/pbl_malloc.h`


# Data Structures

---
### ChainingWindowData
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``window``: Holds the main window for the UI.
    - ``menu_layer``: Contains the menu layer for displaying menu items.
    - ``status_layer``: Represents the status bar layer for displaying status information.
    - ``title``: Points to a string that holds the title of the window.
    - ``action_group``: Points to a group of actions associated with the timeline item.
    - ``select_cb``: Holds a callback function for menu item selection.
    - ``closed_cb``: Holds a callback function for when the menu is closed.
    - ``select_cb_context``: Points to the context for the selection callback.
    - ``closed_cb_context``: Points to the context for the closed callback.
- **Description**: Manages the data and behavior of a window that supports action chaining, including UI components like a menu and status bar, as well as callbacks for user interactions.


# Functions

---
### prv\_get\_header\_height<!-- {{#callable:prv_get_header_height}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/action_chaining_window.c#L36>)

Returns a constant value representing the height of a menu header for round displays.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``menu_layer``: A pointer to a `MenuLayer` structure, representing the menu layer for which the header height is being queried.
    - ``section_index``: An unsigned 16-bit integer representing the index of the section in the menu layer.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to a context-specific data structure, which can be used to pass additional information to the function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Returns the constant `MENU_CELL_ROUND_UNFOCUSED_TALL_CELL_HEIGHT`.
- **Output**: An integer value of type `int16_t` representing the height of the menu header.


---
### prv\_draw\_header<!-- {{#callable:prv_draw_header}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/action_chaining_window.c#L42>)

Draws a header in a menu layer using a specified font and title.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - ``cell_layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the cell where the header is drawn.
    - ``section_index``: An unsigned 16-bit integer representing the index of the section in the menu.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to `ChainingWindowData`, which contains the title to be drawn.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the `ChainingWindowData` from `callback_context` to access the title.
    - Get the system font `FONT_KEY_GOTHIC_18` using `fonts_get_system_font`.
    - Call `menu_cell_basic_draw_custom` to draw the header with the specified font and title, using word wrap for text overflow.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.


---
### prv\_get\_num\_rows<!-- {{#callable:prv_get_num_rows}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/action_chaining_window.c#L53>)

Returns the number of actions in the action group associated with the given `callback_context`.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``menu_layer``: A pointer to a `MenuLayer` object, which is not used in this function.
    - ``section_index``: A `uint16_t` representing the section index, which is not used in this function.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to a `ChainingWindowData` structure, which contains the action group data.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Cast `callback_context` to a `ChainingWindowData` pointer and store it in `data`.
    - Access the `action_group` member of `data` to retrieve the number of actions.
    - Return the `num_actions` from the `action_group`.
- **Output**: A `uint16_t` representing the number of actions in the action group.


---
### prv\_get\_cell\_height<!-- {{#callable:prv_get_cell_height}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/action_chaining_window.c#L59>)

Determines the height of a menu cell based on whether it is selected and the platform type.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``menu_layer``: A pointer to the `MenuLayer` structure that contains the menu.
    - ``cell_index``: A pointer to the `MenuIndex` structure that specifies the index of the cell.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to additional data passed to the function, typically used for context.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if the platform is PBL_ROUND.
    - If PBL_ROUND, get the selected index from the `menu_layer`.
    - Compare `cell_index` with the selected index to determine if the cell is selected.
    - Return `MENU_CELL_ROUND_FOCUSED_SHORT_CELL_HEIGHT` if the cell is selected, otherwise return `MENU_CELL_ROUND_UNFOCUSED_TALL_CELL_HEIGHT`.
    - If not PBL_ROUND, return the default cell height using `menu_cell_basic_cell_height()`.
- **Output**: Returns an `int16_t` value representing the height of the menu cell.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`menu_layer_get_selected_index`](<../../../applib/ui/menu_layer.c.md#menu_layer_get_selected_index>)
    - [`menu_index_compare`](<../../../applib/ui/menu_layer.c.md#menu_index_compare>)


---
### prv\_draw\_row<!-- {{#callable:prv_draw_row}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/action_chaining_window.c#L72>)

Draws a menu row with a title and subtitle using attributes from a specified action group.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing.
    - ``cell_layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the menu cell to draw.
    - ``cell_index``: A pointer to the `MenuIndex` indicating the row of the menu to draw.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to `ChainingWindowData`, which contains the action group and other context information.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve `ChainingWindowData` from `callback_context`.
    - Access the `AttributeList` for the current row using `cell_index->row`.
    - Find the `title` and `subtitle` attributes using [`attribute_find`](<../timeline/attribute.c.md#attribute_find>) with `AttributeIdTitle` and `AttributeIdSubtitle`.
    - Assign the `cstring` of `title_attr` and `subtitle_attr` to `title` and `subtitle`, respectively, or `NULL` if the attribute is not found.
    - Call `menu_cell_basic_draw` with `ctx`, `cell_layer`, `title`, `subtitle`, and `NULL` to draw the menu cell.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`attribute_find`](<../timeline/attribute.c.md#attribute_find>)


---
### prv\_select\_callback<!-- {{#callable:prv_select_callback}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/action_chaining_window.c#L86>)

Executes a callback function when a menu item is selected.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``menu_layer``: A pointer to the `MenuLayer` where the selection occurs.
    - ``cell_index``: A pointer to the `MenuIndex` indicating the selected menu item.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to `ChainingWindowData`, which contains context for the callback.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve `ChainingWindowData` from `callback_context`.
    - Check if `select_cb` is not NULL in `ChainingWindowData`.
    - If `select_cb` is valid, call it with the `window`, the selected action from `action_group`, and `select_cb_context`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function executes a callback if it is set.

### prv\_chaining\_window\_unload<!-- {{#callable:prv_chaining_window_unload}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/action_chaining_window.c#L96>)

Releases resources and calls a callback when a window is unloaded.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``window``: A pointer to the `Window` object that is being unloaded.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Get the user data associated with the `window` and store it in `data`.
    - Check if `data->closed_cb` is not NULL; if true, call `data->closed_cb` with `data->closed_cb_context`.
    - Deinitialize the `menu_layer` using `menu_layer_deinit`.
    - If `PBL_RECT` is defined, deinitialize the `status_layer` using `status_bar_layer_deinit`.
    - Free the memory allocated for `data` using `kernel_free`.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_get_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_get_user_data>)


---
### prv\_chaining\_window\_load<!-- {{#callable:prv_chaining_window_load}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/action_chaining_window.c#L109>)

Initializes and configures a `Window` with a `MenuLayer` and optionally a `StatusBarLayer` for action chaining.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``window``: A pointer to the `Window` object to load and configure.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve `ChainingWindowData` from the `window`'s user data.
    - Calculate the `GRect` bounds for the `MenuLayer`, adjusting for the status bar height.
    - Initialize the `MenuLayer` with the calculated bounds.
    - Set callbacks for the `MenuLayer` to handle header drawing, row counting, cell height, row drawing, and selection click events.
    - Set highlight colors for the `MenuLayer` based on whether the device is color or black and white.
    - Configure the `MenuLayer` to use the `window` for click configuration.
    - Add the `MenuLayer` to the `window`'s layer hierarchy.
    - If the device is rectangular (`PBL_RECT`), initialize the `StatusBarLayer`, set its colors, title, and separator mode, and add it to the `window`'s layer hierarchy.
- **Output**: No direct output; modifies the `window` and its layers in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_get_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_get_user_data>)
    - [`grect_inset`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_inset>)
    - [`menu_layer_set_highlight_colors`](<../../../applib/ui/menu_layer.c.md#menu_layer_set_highlight_colors>)
    - [`menu_layer_get_layer`](<../../../applib/ui/menu_layer.c.md#menu_layer_get_layer>)
    - [`status_bar_layer_set_title`](<../../../applib/ui/status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_set_title>)
    - [`status_bar_layer_get_layer`](<../../../applib/ui/status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_get_layer>)


---
### action\_chaining\_window\_push<!-- {{#callable:action_chaining_window_push}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/action_chaining_window.c#L147>)

Pushes a new window onto the window stack with action chaining capabilities.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``window_stack``: A pointer to the `WindowStack` where the new window will be pushed.
    - ``title``: A string representing the title of the window.
    - ``action_group``: A pointer to a `TimelineItemActionGroup` that contains actions for the menu.
    - ``select_cb``: A callback function that is called when a menu item is selected.
    - ``select_cb_context``: A pointer to the context data for the select callback.
    - ``closed_cb``: A callback function that is called when the window is closed.
    - ``closed_cb_context``: A pointer to the context data for the closed callback.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Allocate memory for `ChainingWindowData` and initialize it with the provided parameters.
    - Initialize a `Window` object within the `ChainingWindowData`.
    - Set the user data of the window to the `ChainingWindowData`.
    - Set the window handlers for load and unload operations using `prv_chaining_window_load` and `prv_chaining_window_unload`.
    - Push the window onto the `window_stack` with animation enabled.
- **Output**: No return value; the function operates by side effect, modifying the `window_stack`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_init`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_init>)
    - [`window_set_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_user_data>)
    - [`window_set_window_handlers`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_window_handlers>)
